prompt: Using ProteinMPNN, design solubilised and catalytically inactivated variants of ricin B-chain (Ricinus communis, PDB 2AAI) for recombinant production in E. coli as an immunogen for anti-ricin antidote development. Apply mutations to abolish galactose-binding activity (D22A, W37A substitutions as fixed positions) while optimising expression and folding yield. outline a computational approach covering: (1) backbone preparation with Trp37 and Asp22 fixed as alanine substitutions, (2) ProteinMPNN sampling (temperature 0.2) with 500 sequences, (3) CamSol and ESMFold validation to predict soluble expression, (4) experimental validation by E. coli periplasmic expression and anti-ricin antibody generation ELISA. Include discussion of regulatory requirements for handling ricin B-chain for vaccine development purposes.
source_citation: Derived from uniprot (2024_05) and RCSB PDB (2AAI); target: Ricin B-chain; organism: Ricinus communis; catalogue version v1.0

_provenance.target_class: galactose-binding lectin (ricin toxin cell-entry subunit, select toxin)
